# Copilot Instructions for MagenSec.github.io

## Project Overview

This is the MagenSec platform website and portal, hosted on GitHub Pages at [magensec.github.io](https://magensec.github.io). It consists of:

- **Marketing/docs site**: Static HTML pages at the root (index.html, about.html, install.html, etc.)
- **Portal**: A single-page application at `/portal/` for device security management and organization administration, live at [magensec.github.io/portal](https://magensec.github.io/portal/)
- **Diagnostic data**: Automated security feed data under `/diag/` (CISA KEV, CVRF)

## Tech Stack

- **Frontend**: Vanilla HTML5, CSS (SASS under `assets/sass/`), vanilla JavaScript (ES modules, no bundler)
- **Portal SPA**: Preact (loaded via CDN), `htm` tagged templates for JSX-like syntax, client-side routing via hash routes (`#!/...`)
- **Scripting/Automation**: PowerShell (`.ps1`) for tooling and GitHub Actions steps
- **Hosting**: GitHub Pages (static files only — no server-side processing)

## Repository Layout

```
.github/workflows/   # GitHub Actions: CISA KEV updates, CVRF parsing, tag management
assets/              # Shared CSS (sass/), JS, images, webfonts for the marketing site
diag/                # Security feed data (CVRF parser script + generated JSON files)
editions/            # Product edition information pages
platform/            # Platform feature pages
portal/              # Portal SPA (see portal/README.md)
  assets/            # Portal-specific static assets
  js/                # All portal JavaScript (ES modules)
    api/             # API client modules
    components/      # Shared UI components
    hooks/           # Preact hooks
    pages/           # Page components, one folder per route
    services/        # Business-logic services (e.g. OrgService.js)
    utils/           # Utility helpers
    app.js           # App entry point / router bootstrap
    router.js        # Hash-based SPA router
  portal.css         # Portal stylesheet
scripts/             # Developer tooling (PowerShell)
  start-dev-server.ps1   # Start local HTTP server on port 8080
  generate-sri.ps1       # Generate SRI hashes for CDN assets
```

## Development Setup

Start a local dev server using PowerShell:

```powershell
./scripts/start-dev-server.ps1
```

The site is then accessible at `http://localhost:8080/` and the portal at `http://localhost:8080/portal/`. Note that Server-Side Includes (SSI) are not processed by the dev server.

There is no build step, bundler, or package manager for the main site or portal. Files are served directly.

## Portal Architecture

- **Framework**: Preact + `htm` (accessed from `window` globals, loaded via CDN script tags in `portal/index.html`)
- **Routing**: Hash-based (`#!/route`) defined in `portal/js/router.js`
- **State**: Component-level `useState`/`useEffect`; shared org context via `portal/js/orgContext.js`
- **API calls**: Centralised in `portal/js/api/` modules; called from page components or services
- **Page pattern**: Each page lives in `portal/js/pages/<page-name>/`, exports a default component

### Portal Code Conventions

- Use `const { html, Component } = window;` and `const { useState, useEffect } = window.preactHooks;` at the top of each portal module
- Write components as functions (not classes) using hooks
- Use `htm` tagged template literals (`html\`...\``) instead of JSX
- Org types are `Personal`, `Education`, `Business`; Demo is a flag (`isDemoOrg`), not a type
- Use `dailyReportEnabled` and `weeklyReportEnabled` for report toggle fields (not legacy variants)
- Resolve org type with the `getOrgType(org)` helper (`org.orgType || org.type || legacy isPersonal`)

## GitHub Actions Workflows

| Workflow | Trigger | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `update-cisa-kev.yml` | Every 6 hours | Downloads CISA KEV JSON → `diag/known_exploited_vulnerabilities.json` |
| `update-cvrf.yml` | Every 6 hours | Runs `diag/cvrf-parser.ps1` → `diag/cvrf_products.json`, `diag/cvrf_cache.json` |
| `update-latest-tag.yml` | Release published | Updates the `latest` git tag |

## Important Notes

- This is a **static site**; do not introduce server-side dependencies or build tools without updating the workflow and deployment configuration
- **No test framework** is currently configured; validate changes by running the dev server and exercising the relevant pages manually
- Avoid committing generated files in `diag/` (they are managed by the automated workflows) unless you are updating the parser logic
- SRI hashes for CDN assets are generated by `scripts/generate-sri.ps1`; regenerate after updating CDN links in HTML files
